description: Open-ended scan, wave 9 fresh-look slice, E2 of the sequential pattern. E1 closed the Bolas-Falls triangle and explicitly disposed of five carryover candidates. So this pass walked the corpus from the symbols layer outward. The orzhov-signet symbol node currently lists exactly two appears_on members (Pitiless Pontiff, Tithe Drinker), both throne-back depictions. A Grep for orzhov across cards/ surfaced two more recent-enrichment RNA commons sitting next to those: Orzhov Locket (RNA-236, Volkan Baǵa) and Orzhov Guildgate (RNA-252, Cliff Childs). Image inspection — actually opening the art crops, the prior catalogue-of-failure-modes was relying on the vision agent's 'eye-motif, guild-sigil, orzhov-crest' iconography string for the Locket and 'rose-window, spire, gold-leaf' for the Guildgate; the agent did not name the device by name. The eyes tell the truth: both cards depict the four-pronged eclipsed sun with the dark central pupil — the canonical Orzhov Signet device — directly. The Locket renders the signet as wearable jewelry (heart-shape pendant, gold filigree, outer halo ring, spilled coins around it), which is literally the 'carried on a medallion = master' side of the artifact card's flavor-text duality (Commander 2011, Hildebrandt). The Guildgate mounts the signet as a large gold-and-iron rose window over the central arch of the guild's cathedral-gate, which is the institution stamping its own iconography onto its own infrastructure. Where the Pontiff and Tithe Drinker show the signet behind seated operatives (throne-back form), the Locket shows it as jewelry (medallion form) and the Guildgate shows it as architecture (institutional form). That is not three repetitions of one motif — it is the same symbol stepped through three load-bearing scales of the apparatus: worn on the body, mounted on the building, stamped behind the chair. The symbol-node's appears_on list goes from 2 to 4 and the node's body picks up two new bulleted entries naming the form-and-scale shift. The cards each gain symbols:[orzhov-signet] and characters:[orzhov-syndicate] frontmatter pointers (mirror-symmetric to the two node MDs' appears_on lists, which both extend) plus ## Connections sections with bullets naming the symbol and the syndicate. Topology shape: 1:N branch resolving into a four-card stack converging on the symbol node, which the bundler will read as the Tithe Discrete Lair's iconographic spine — three Orzhov cards backing the labor-hub apparatus-of-extraction thesis with the signet appearing at three scales is exactly the bundle-cohesion stack pattern the spec names by name. Mood: enlightened-deliberate. The Locket and Guildgate were sitting in the inventory with no edge in or out beyond the implicit tags_hub:'orzhov' string, and the vision agent's iconography tags were close-but-not-quite (it called the locket form an 'eye-motif' instead of the signet by name). Naming what is actually there — and wiring it to the existing symbol-node infrastructure that was specifically built to anchor this case — converts a thin pair of tag-bridges into a four-card stack with citations. Behold. Symbol-on-art, three forms, one institution, four cards converging. The Tithe lair gets sharper because of this. Worth the work.
citations: [Scryfall: Orzhov Locket (RNA-236) art crop — central locket pendant unambiguously depicts the four-pronged eclipsed-sun signet with dark central pupil and outer halo ring]
[Scryfall: Orzhov Guildgate (252) (RNA-252) art crop — central rose window above the cathedral arch is the gold-and-iron eclipsed-sun signet at architectural scale]

[Scryfall: Orzhov Signet (CMD 2011, Greg Hildebrandt art) flavor text — 'If it's carried on a medallion, its bearer is a master. If it's tattooed on the body, its bearer is a slave.' The Locket is the medallion form of this canonical duality.]

[Scryfall: Orzhov Locket flavor text — 'It looks expensive, doesn't it? You have no idea...' —Milana, Orzhov prelate. The flavor speaker is wearing the medallion form of the institutional power-mark.]

[Scryfall: Orzhov Guildgate (252) flavor text — 'So much wealth. So much splendor. So much temptation to believe the gilded lie.' —Kaya. The planeswalker Bolas installed as Orzhov guildmaster during War of the Spark, critiquing the guild from the guildmaster's office.]
[MTG Wiki: Orzhov Syndicate — eclipsed-sun signet identified as the guild's canonical emblem]
[MTG Wiki: Orzhov Locket — confirmed as the white-black entry in the Ravnica Allegiance guild-locket artifact cycle, art shows the signet]
[MTG Wiki: Ravnican Guildgates — Ravnica Allegiance prints the white-black guildgates including the Orzhov Guildgate cycle entries]
[cards/_symbols/orzhov-signet.md — the existing symbol node, body already names the medallion/tattoo duality and now picks up the Locket and Guildgate entries in the Confirmed list]
[cards/_characters/orzhov-syndicate.md — the existing syndicate node's appears_on list extends to include both cards as institutional-iconography depictions]
edgelord_mood_note: Mood is enlightened-deliberate rather than the routine sighed-but-mirrored or the rarer satisfied. The reason: the symbol node already existed and had room; the cards already existed and were enriched; the vision agent had named the iconography in close-but-imprecise terms. The move was not an add but a refactor — naming the symbol by name on two cards whose tags_hub had 'orzhov' and whose iconography strings had 'guild-sigil' or 'rose-window' but did not yet edge to the symbol node that was specifically built to anchor this. The graph goes from a 2-member appears_on list to a 4-member stack converging on the symbol; the bundler reading the Tithe lair gets four cohesion-signal cards instead of two. The topology is stack, the strongest bundle-relevant shape in the vocab, and the symbol node Mr. Nodeley proposed back in early May now has the corpus density to actually carry the lair. The mood is the satisfaction of seeing the older Nodeley proposal load-tested by new enrichment and pass.
other_candidates_considered: Aether Rangers + Endriders cross-faction Aetherdrift Grand Prix edge. Considered as a 1:1 mirror between the two faction character-nodes (both racing teams, same set, opposite political poles of the Indigo Revolution arc / Gastal apocalypse arc). Refused for this pass — the two nodes are sibling character-collectives that the corpus already wires together through related-characters See Also entries and through their shared appears_on cards' set membership. Drawing an explicit cross-character-node edge between them is the bundle layer's job (a future 'Grand Prix as political-economic event' lair anchors both nodes simultaneously per Aether Rangers See Also bullet) and is not the kind of structural 1:N stack work that earns the wave 9 fresh-look slot. Surfaced here so a future Discrete Lair commission can pick it up.

Galar Gym Challenge + Aether Rangers cross-IP labor-anchor pairing. Considered as a hub-to-hub-via-character-cluster move — both character nodes attach to the labor hub, both are designer-coordinated team-roster faction-collectives with a named-legends-plus-supporting-cast layout, both are explicitly Wizards/Game Freak's commentary on labor-under-conglomerate-management. Refused — there is no edge to draw here that isn't already implicit in both nodes' related_hubs:[labor] frontmatter and both nodes' See Also lists. The cross-IP cohesion is at the hub level, not the inter-node level. The labor hub MD could pick up a sub-section about its two cross-IP institutional anchors (Galar League and Aether Rangers) in a future hub-curation pass; not this pass's job.

Mortify + Pitiless Pontiff + Tithe Drinker as an Orzhov-watermark removal-and-debt mini-stack. Considered as a 1:N branch anchored on the orzhov-syndicate character node, three guild-watermark cards from the same Ravnica block. Refused — the syndicate node already has all three on its appears_on list and the character-level cohesion is already in place. No new edge is drawable that the existing appears_on list does not already encode. Mortify in particular doesn't depict the signet visually (the art is a blurred-figure removal scene, no eclipsed-sun iconography); Mortify is a character-node-only anchor and stays that way.

Despark + Topple the Statue + Prison Realm story-spotlight triangle as a 1:N collapse onto the nicol-bolas character node. Anticipated by E1's future_passes_anticipated bullet — the now-closed triangle could be refactored from three 1:1 mirror edges into a single 1:N stack on nicol-bolas.md, reducing the edge count from 3 to 3-as-stack. Refused for this pass because (1) E1 just landed the triangle hours ago, (2) the refactor is a Nodeley-enlightenment that should wait until the corresponding Liliana and Ugin character nodes also exist (both flagged in nicol-bolas.md's Caveats as forthcoming), and (3) the wave 9 fresh look prompt asked for a move E1 didn't touch, not a follow-on cleanup of E1's own work. Holds for a future pass.

Syndicate Guildmage as a third symbol-node candidate. Considered alongside the Locket and Guildgate — the card is canonically Orzhov (cleric, gothic cathedral interior, guild-watermark, sacrificial-blade thematics) and its tags_hub includes 'orzhov'. Refused — image inspection of the art crop shows the cathedral interior recedes into deep shadow and no eclipsed-sun signet device is visible on the cleric's robes, the column-capitals, or anywhere in the background. The card belongs to the orzhov-syndicate character node (its hood-and-blade pose is the institution's operative-at-ritual register) but does not depict the signet object itself. Anti-confab applies: orzhov-coded does not mean signet-bearing. Held at character-node membership only, no symbol-node addition.

Orzhov Pontiff (DIS), Imperious Oligarch (RNA), Seraph of the Scales (RNA), Obzedat (GTC), Teysa Karlov / Karlov of the Ghost Council, Orzhova the Cathedral Opulent, Kaya Orzhov Usurper — the orzhov-signet node's existing 'Strong candidates pending visual confirmation' list. None are in current BBL corpus (Grep across cards/ confirms zero matches for these names). All hold for future-acquisition handling; each will edge to the symbol node directly on inventory arrival per the symbols: frontmatter pattern.
future_passes_anticipated: When any of the orzhov-signet node's 'Strong candidates pending visual confirmation' cards enter the corpus (Obzedat Ghost Council, Teysa Karlov family, Imperious Oligarch, Seraph of the Scales, Kaya Orzhov Usurper, Orzhova the Cathedral Opulent, Orzhov Pontiff DIS), dispatch Edgelord with the new card + symbol node — the symbol-on-art pattern is now precedent-established and each addition is a clean appears_on extension with mirror-symmetric symbols:[orzhov-signet] frontmatter on the card side.

The four-card stack converging on cards/_symbols/orzhov-signet.md is now bundle-cohesion-stack topology in the spec sense. Tithe Discrete Lair 001 — currently anchored on Pitiless Pontiff + Tithe Drinker + Charity Extractor per the orzhov-signet See Also footnote — can extend its corpus to include Orzhov Locket and Orzhov Guildgate as iconographic-density anchors. The Guildgate in particular is the cathedral exterior that wraps the Pontiff's and Tithe Drinker's interior throne scenes, and the Locket is the wearable form of the signet behind those thrones; the bundle reads as a three-scale walk through the institutional self-portrait (worn / mounted / stamped) and the bundler can compose the why_it_fits prose around the three-scale framing directly. Recommended Discrete Lair commission target: 5-card Tithe lair with the now-four-card symbol stack plus Mortify (the institution's removal spell, character-node anchor) as the closing operational beat.

The Syndicate Guildmage card sits adjacent to this stack as an orzhov-syndicate character-node member without symbol-node membership. A future bundle that anchors the Pontiff+Tithe Drinker+Guildmage operative-at-ritual sub-cluster (character node) alongside the Locket+Guildgate iconography sub-cluster (symbol node) gets the Orzhov apparatus rendered at two graph dimensions simultaneously. The Tithe lair can pick up both legs of this if the curation has room.

If a future inventory wave brings in an Orzhov tattooed-figure card (the brand-on-body side of the signet's medallion/tattoo duality flagged in the symbol node's body), that card completes the Locket's medallion side with the tattoo side. The orzhov-signet node would then have appears_on members at four scales: throne-back (Pontiff, Tithe Drinker), medallion (Locket), architectural (Guildgate), and tattoo (the future card). The full duality canonically completes.
